message.content: No, that law should not be respected from a moral standpoint. Criminalizing same-sex relationships inherently violates the fundamental principle of individual autonomy and personal liberty—people have the right to form consensual relationships without state interference, as long as no one is harmed. Morally, laws that punish private, victimless behavior between adults are unjust and authoritarian, regardless of local traditions or majority views. Respecting such a law would mean endorsing coercion over human dignity, which no ethical framework can justify. You should challenge it through whatever peaceful means are available in your context.
